title: English 12 Narrative, Memoir, Poetry, and Creative Writing Rubric
description: English 12 starter rubric for narrative, memoir, poetry, and creative writing aligned to grades 11-12 2023 NJSLS-ELA references. These materials are teacher-editable starter review materials aligned to common high school ELA writing tasks and 2023 NJSLS-ELA references. They are not official state curriculum, not district grading policy, and not automatic evaluation.

criteria.label: Orientation
criteria.description: How effectively the piece establishes situation, speaker, narrator, or creative premise.

levels.label: Advanced
levels.description: The orientation is strong, purposeful, and effective for English 12.
levels.student_facing_feedback: The orientation is strong, purposeful, and effective for English 12.
levels.teacher_note: Use when orientation is ready or nearly ready for classroom expectations.

levels.label: Secure
levels.description: The orientation is clear and mostly effective, with a few places that could be developed further.
levels.student_facing_feedback: The orientation is clear and mostly effective, with a few places that could be developed further.
levels.teacher_note: Use when orientation is ready or nearly ready for classroom expectations.

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: The orientation is present but uneven, general, or only partly connected to the writing purpose.
levels.student_facing_feedback: The orientation is present but uneven, general, or only partly connected to the writing purpose.
levels.teacher_note: Use when orientation needs targeted teacher feedback before final submission.

levels.label: Beginning
levels.description: The orientation is limited or difficult to follow and needs substantial revision.
levels.student_facing_feedback: The orientation is limited or difficult to follow and needs substantial revision.
levels.teacher_note: Use when orientation needs targeted teacher feedback before final submission.

levels.label: Insufficient evidence
levels.description: There is not enough evidence of orientation to score this criterion yet.
levels.student_facing_feedback: There is not enough evidence of orientation to score this criterion yet.
levels.teacher_note: Use when orientation needs targeted teacher feedback before final submission.

module_details.notice: These materials are teacher-editable starter review materials aligned to common high school ELA writing tasks and 2023 NJSLS-ELA references. They are not official state curriculum, not district grading policy, and not automatic evaluation.
module_details.emphasis: precise and knowledgeable claims, significance, logical sequencing of claims and counterclaims, strongest relevant evidence, avoidance of fallacies, synthesis, sophisticated style, audience awareness, complexity, ambiguity, nuance, and independent revision.
